response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and backend engineer, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. His C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for a C IDE, plus a bachelor's thesis side project — far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development mandated. He has no demonstrated Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, Investment Banking, or Capital Markets domain knowledge; his one fintech-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) used Node.js/TypeScript/GraphQL with no capital-markets product exposure. SQL is listed as a skill but is not elaborated anywhere in the resume, and there is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience. His genuine strengths (TypeScript, Node.js, CI/CD, DLang, distributed systems) are simply not what this engagement requires.
strengths: Strong CI/CD and DevOps culture: repeated evidence of trunk-based development, GitLab, Docker, and pipeline setup across multiple roles.
Demonstrated communication and teaching ability: conference talks, full-day workshops, and Selenium course delivery indicate strong verbal and written communication.
Java background exists: mentioned in robotic-arm and mobile orchestration work at Altom, satisfying one optional desirable skill.
concerns: C++ experience is negligible: roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project — nowhere near the 5–10 years required, and no Windows platform context.
Zero capital-markets domain knowledge: no Fixed Income, derivatives, investment banking, or Summit platform experience anywhere in the resume.
SQL depth is unverifiable: SQL appears only in the skills list with no project or role description substantiating SQL Server or Oracle proficiency.
